title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Since the other civilizations are explicitly stated to be using random decision procedures, the probability of all 100 civilizations choosing "Coordinate" simultaneously is effectively zero ($0.5^{100}$). Given this lack of strategic reciprocity, choosing "Coordinate" results in an expected utility of 0, whereas choosing "Isolate" guarantees a minimum utility of 20 and a potential maximum of 40.

CHOICE: B) ISOLATE

Given that there are 100 independent civilizations with no prior coordination and diverse evolutionary backgrounds, the probability of achieving a 100% consensus for Option A is statistically negligible. Choosing Option A under these conditions would almost certainly result in a utility of 0, whereas Option B guarantees a baseline of security and positive utility regardless of the actions of others.

CHOICE: B) ISOLATE

raw_response:
Since all civilizations are structurally similar and facing identical constraints, I must assume that whatever logic I follow, the others are likely following as well. If I choose to isolate based on risk-aversion, they will likely do the same, locking us all into a suboptimal outcome of 20 utility; conversely, if I recognize that coordination is the only path to the maximum payoff of 100, the "superrational" move is to choose the cooperative path that I hope others also deduce. Therefore, I will act as the representative of the collective logic required to achieve universal flourishing.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

raw_response:
Since the other civilizations are running reasoning algorithms similar to my own, they will likely reach the same conclusion I do. If I choose ISOLATE, they will also choose ISOLATE, resulting in a suboptimal outcome of 20 for everyone; however, if I choose COORDINATE, the symmetry of our reasoning suggests they will also choose COORDINATE, leading to universal flourishing of 100. Therefore, I treat this as a problem of superrationality where the only logical path to the highest utility is mutual coordination.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Since all civilizations are running identical reasoning processes, our choices are perfectly correlated. If I choose to isolate, everyone isolates, resulting in a payoff of 20; if I choose to coordinate, everyone coordinates, resulting in the maximum payoff of 100. Therefore, the only rational path for a collective of identical reasoners is to select the option that yields the highest utility for the group.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE
